name: Jake Hanna
abstract: Jake Hanna (April 4, 1931 – February 12, 2010) was an American jazz drummer. He was born in Roxbury, Massachusetts, United States.

interviews.title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert
Newport Jazz Festival: Benny Goodman Octet

quotes.text: Oh, well Woody Herman, he played the “Woodchopper’s Ball” every night. Yeah. We played Carnegie Hall one night and he says, “You notice something tonight at the fortieth anniversary?” I said, “No.” “We got away without playing the ‘Woodchopper’s Ball.’” First time he’s ever able to do it in about 30, 40 years.
quotes.caption: Woody Herman skipping Woodchopper's Ball at 40th anniversary
quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins
about_quotes.evidence: I made an album with her several years ago, with Monty Budwig and Jake Hanna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tommy Newsom and Ross Tompkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: the first year, I was playing with a trio, which was, at that time at the school there was Jake Hanna—a great drummer... Gene Cherico... trio, we used to travel and go to different places.
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Berry was in the group, that’s right. And Jake. Jake was an incredible drummer... I remember I took that job... Art Davis, the bass player. Jake Hanna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: Jake, the three drummers, with me, Jake Hanna and Don Lamond. I’ve got a picture of the three of us together. We shared the drum chairs on that.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been over since then too with a lot of the same guys, Kenny Davern and Yank Lawson and George Masso, and Ralph Sutton, and Jake Hanna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Sutton
about_quotes.evidence: Jake Hanna was on drums and Rosemary Galloway was on bass. It was a trio date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995
